prediction:
FROM customers c
| JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id
| WHERE o.status = 'paid'
| GROUP c.customer_id, c.customer_name
| AGG c.customer_id, c.customer_name, COUNT(o.order_id) AS paid_order_count
| ORDER paid_order_count DESC, c.customer_id ASC
repair_actions: Added an ORDER step to sort customers by their paid order count in descending order (and by customer_id for deterministic tie-breaking).

prediction:
FROM customers c
| GROUP c.level
| AGG c.level, COUNT(c.customer_id) AS customer_count
| ORDER customer_count DESC, c.level
repair_actions: Added an ORDER step to enforce deterministic ordering.
Order by the aggregate alias customer_count in descending order (largest counts first) and then by c.level ascending to break ties.

prediction:
FROM orders o
| JOIN order_items oi ON oi.order_id = o.order_id
| WHERE o.status = 'paid'
| GROUP o.order_id
| AGG o.order_id, SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price) AS order_amount
| ORDER order_amount DESC
repair_actions: Changed ORDER from ordering by o.order_id ASC to ordering by the aggregate alias order_amount DESC to provide a deterministic, meaningful sort (largest order amounts first).

prediction:
FROM customers c
| JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id
| JOIN order_items oi ON o.order_id = oi.order_id
| JOIN products p ON oi.product_id = p.product_id
| GROUP c.customer_id, c.customer_name
| AGG COUNT(DISTINCT p.category) AS category_count
| SELECT c.customer_id, c.customer_name, category_count
| ORDER category_count DESC
repair_actions: Moved non-aggregate grouping columns out of the AGG step into a SELECT step.
Changed AGG step to include only the aggregate expression COUNT(DISTINCT p.category) AS category_count.
Kept the GROUP and JOIN structure unchanged and preserved the ORDER by category_count DESC.
